prefuel.title: Pre-race fueling
prefuel.days57.title: 5–7 days out
prefuel.days57.body: No dramatic diet changes. Keep eating the foods you train on — your body knows them. Focus on consistent meals, good sleep, and staying hydrated throughout the week.
prefuel.days57.carb: Start slightly increasing your carbohydrate portions from 3 days out — add an extra serving of rice, pasta, or oats to dinner. This tops up muscle glycogen without needing a full carb-load protocol.
prefuel.dayBefore.title: Day before
prefuel.dayBefore.shortBody: No carb-loading needed for this distance. Eat a normal, familiar dinner — something you've had before training. Avoid anything heavy, rich, or new. Drink plenty of water through the day.
prefuel.dayBefore.medBody: Focus on carbohydrates at dinner: rice, pasta, or potato. Avoid high-fat or high-fibre foods that might cause gut issues on race day.
prefuel.dayBefore.longBody: This distance benefits from a proper carb-load. Aim for ~8–10g of carbohydrate per kg of body weight across the day — mostly from rice, pasta, bread, or oats. Keep fat and fibre low. Drink 2–3L of water. Lay out your race kit and nutrition in the evening.
prefuel.morning.title: Race morning
prefuel.morning.body: Eat your main pre-race meal 2.5–3 hours before your start. Aim for 60–90g of carbohydrates — a bowl of oats with banana and honey, white rice with a little salt, or toast with jam work well. Keep it simple and familiar.
prefuel.morning.longExtra: For a race this long, also drink 500ml of water with a pinch of salt when you wake up. This helps prime blood plasma volume before you even start.
prefuel.before.title: 30–60 min before start
prefuel.before.body: In the 30–60 minutes before your start, have a Lecka bar or half a gel with 200–300ml of water. This gives your body a final carbohydrate top-up without sitting heavy. Avoid eating within 15 minutes of the gun — the insulin response can cause a brief energy dip early in the race.
prefuel.before.warmExtra: In warm conditions, also sip an extra 300–500ml of water or electrolyte drink in this window.
share.emailBody: Check out my race nutrition plan:
share.useWithGels: Use with any real food gel matching these targets
claude.heading: Lecka Nutrition Tools for Claude
claude.body: Lecka's product catalog and race fueling calculator are available as tools inside Claude. Ask about carb targets, product recommendations, or race-day strategy — Claude will call Lecka's data directly and give you a personalised answer.
claude.example1: What Lecka gels work for a hot marathon?
claude.example2: I'm 70kg, racing a 70.3 in 5h30. How many gels do I need?
claude.example3: Which Lecka products are vegan and gluten-free?
claude.example4: What's the sodium content of the Ultra Gel?
claude.setupInstructions: In Claude, go to Customize → Connectors → + → Add Manual Connector and paste the URL above.
claude.reconnectNote: If the tools stop responding mid-conversation, go to Settings → Integrations and reconnect the Lecka server. This is a known Claude limitation with remote connections.
claude.freeNote: Lecka MCP tools are free to use. Requires a Claude account.
